description: Experiments configuration for Witch's Brew / Potion Game. Each entry defines what ingredients exist, what happens when specific combinations are added to the cauldron, and what the child observes. There are no questions — the learning comes from experimenting freely and watching the outcomes. The game engine handles all animation and presentation; this file is the source of truth for ingredient properties and reaction logic.

ingredients.notes: Base liquid for most Band 2 potions. Neutral — no reaction on its own.
Does not mix with water — demonstrates liquid immiscibility.
Interchangeable with dragon tears. Used to vary visual presentation.
Murky appearance teaches that not all liquids are clear.
Floats on water-based liquids. Used for density/layering experiments in Band 3.
Creates rainbow-shimmer visual effect. Used in light-themed experiments.
Dissolves in dragon tears or enchanted water to form a clear solution.
Dissolves in warm water. Demonstrates solution formation.
Dissolves and colours the liquid visibly — good visual reinforcement of dissolving.
Does not dissolve — creates a suspension that settles over time.
Suspends in water briefly then settles — classic suspension demonstration.
Sinks immediately and does not dissolve or suspend. Used to contrast with dissolving solids.
Reacts with acidic liquids (moonberry juice) to produce visible bubbles (CO2). Do not combine with plain water — no reaction.
Acidic liquid. Reacts with fizzing powder. Also acts as a pH indicator — turns blue_lotus_powder red.

Dissolves in water to give blue colour. Turns red/pink in acidic conditions (moonberry juice). Turns green in alkaline conditions (stardust solution). Teaches pH indicator concept without the chemistry label.
Dissolves to create an alkaline solution. Turns blue_lotus_powder green. Neutralises moonberry juice reaction.
Narrative ingredient for sound experiments. Dissolves in water and triggers a sound wave visual effect when combined with heat.
Dissolves and causes a glowing light effect. Brighter glow than moonstone powder — used in variable-testing experiments.
Dissolves and produces a dim glow. Contrast ingredient for sun crystal variable tests.

reactions.description: Star dust dissolves completely — the cauldron liquid becomes clear and slightly shimmery.
reactions.real_world_concept: dissolution — a solid forming a solution in a liquid

reactions.description: Dragon scales dissolve to create a shimmering teal solution.
Crystal powder dissolves and turns the water a bright purple.
Fine mud disperses throughout the cauldron making it cloudy. After a few seconds, particles slowly settle to the bottom.
reactions.real_world_concept: suspension — solid particles that do not dissolve and eventually settle

reactions.description: The toad stone sinks immediately to the bottom. The water stays clear. Nothing dissolves.
reactions.real_world_concept: insoluble solid — does not dissolve or suspend

reactions.description: The oil floats on top of the water in a distinct layer — the two liquids do not mix.
reactions.real_world_concept: immiscible liquids — two liquids that do not mix

reactions.description: The cauldron fizzes and bubbles dramatically! A gas is produced. The mixture froths.
reactions.real_world_concept: chemical reaction — acid + base → gas (CO2) produced

reactions.description: The cauldron turns a deep blue. The potion is now a colour-changing indicator.
The blue potion turns bright red/pink when moonberry juice is added — showing an acidic condition.
reactions.real_world_concept: pH indicator in acid — colour change signals acidity

reactions.description: The blue potion turns green when stardust is added — showing an alkaline condition.
reactions.real_world_concept: pH indicator in alkali — colour change signals alkalinity

reactions.description: The volcano oil floats on top in a distinct red layer. The two liquids form visible density layers.
reactions.real_world_concept: density — less dense liquids float on more dense liquids

reactions.description: The cauldron glows brightly in gold — sun crystal dissolved and released strong light energy.
reactions.real_world_concept: variable testing — sun crystal produces more light than moonstone

reactions.description: The cauldron glows dimly in silver — moonstone powder released less light energy than sun crystal.
reactions.real_world_concept: variable testing — contrast result for sun crystal comparison

reactions.description: The sound stone dissolves and the cauldron surface ripples with visible sound waves radiating outward.
reactions.real_world_concept: sound travels as waves through a medium

reactions.description: Just water in the cauldron. Nothing happens yet.
reactions.real_world_concept: baseline state — liquid in a container

potions.description: A clear, shimmering potion that is barely visible in the cauldron.
potions.curriculum_concept: A solid dissolving completely in a liquid forms a clear solution.

potions.description: A murky, green-brown potion with particles that slowly settle to the bottom.
potions.curriculum_concept: A suspension — solid particles that do not dissolve and eventually settle.

potions.description: A fizzing, bubbling potion that overflows with foam.
potions.curriculum_concept: A chemical reaction between an acid and a base produces a gas.

potions.description: A potion that changes colour depending on what is added — blue normally, red with moonberry juice, green with stardust.
potions.curriculum_concept: A pH indicator changes colour in different conditions, showing cause and effect.

potions.description: A glowing potion — brighter with sun crystal, dimmer with moonstone. Good for variable comparison.
potions.curriculum_concept: Changing one ingredient (the variable) produces a measurably different outcome.

potions.description: A potion that hums and ripples — showing sound waves moving through the liquid.
potions.curriculum_concept: Sound travels as waves through a medium (the liquid).
